column7: I love that Acumatica is truly cloud-based, making accessibility seamless for our global teams. The generic inquiry (GI) feature is a game changer, allowing even non-technical users like me to build custom reports and dashboards easily. Its intuitive web interface makes onboarding new employees quick, and the scalability means we can grow without being handcuffed by the ERP system. The consumption-based pricing structure is also a huge plus, and the reliability has been outstanding compared to previous systems.
column8: Honestly, there isn’t much to dislike, but the process of migrating from our old system was complex, especially consolidating customers from multiple databases. Integrating new acquisitions and handling multi-currency, multi-tenant setups required extra effort and some creative workarounds, but Acumatica’s flexibility helped us address these challenges. I’d also say that while AI features are promising, we’re still exploring their full potential.
column10: Scalability and flexibility are the biggest strengths for us. Because of features like attributes, we’ve been able to design the underlying data structure so it can live in both big and small buckets, depending on what we need. That makes it easy to add something like a brand attribute for a new product, and then have the data automatically flow throughout the data ecosystem we’ve created. Acumatica Delivers Flexibility and Scalability for

column7:
Customer Centric Mentality. Acumatica is the most adaptable ERP System I have ever had the pleasure to introduce to the Companies I have worked for. The sheer ease of use, ease of training and the overall homey feel of Acumatica comes top of mind.

It has been clearly built for the Cloud by Accounting, Operations, Procurement, Sales and Admin people on the Team. Every detail has been thoroughly tested and where there is a shortfall, Acumatica takes Ownership and works to clean whatever Issue up quickly and to the Customer's Satisfaction.
column8: So far, our Support Team hasn't told us "NO" to any request or scenario we have asked to implement. It is our mission to someday stump the Team, but so far Acumatica has handled all of our requests with no or minimal enhancements.
column10: Our ROI is incalculable. I like hearing our various Team members say, "That was easy..." when training or showing them how to do something in Acumatica. Streamlined processes have saved us so much time in operations that we have time on our hands to better serve our Customers, Vendors, Stakeholders. Acumatica: Adaptable, Easy-to-Train Cloud ERP with

column7:
We’ve had a largely positive experience with Acumatica. The platform is reasonably priced, especially given the breadth of functionality it offers. It’s also highly customizable and scalable, which has allowed it to fit the specific needs of our industry and continue to adapt as our organization grows.

A major advantage for our team has been how well Acumatica integrates with Excel and Velixo. Being able to import and export data easily, build reports, and work within a familiar Excel environment has made our financial reporting and analysis much more efficient.

We’ve also found the support team to be responsive and helpful, which matters a lot when implementing new workflows or refining existing ones in a system as robust as an ERP.

Overall, Acumatica has proven to be a flexible, reliable platform that supports both day-to-day operations and longer-term growth.
column8: Definitely not "plug and play", formal training is necessary and the system will take time to learn.
column10: Accurate reporting and an efficient system. Flexible, Scalable ERP with Strong Excel/Velixo Integration and

column7: I really appreciate that Acumatica supports our entire business from lead management to sales orders and fulfillment. I find it flexible, easy to use, yet operationally robust and functional. It allows us to replicate the business processes that the market demands and to do so in a functionally correct manner.
column8: The richness of the product requires more serious support from Acumatica that we seem to have difficulty accessing, at times. We currently use Acumatica as our VAR, and they have structural issues that have led to a negative experience. The transition from Sage 100 was initially painful, taking 3 years to convert. Challenges came from end user non-participation and lack of serious commitment.
column10: Acumatica supports our business from lead management to payment collection. It's flexible and easy to use yet robust, and it lets us adapt to market demands in a functionally correct manner. Flexible ERP with Robust Features, But Needs

column7: Its easy to navigate and use, new users didnt need much training to get comfortable with it. Its not the cheapest but its been worth it for the visibility and time saving.
column8:
Pricing can add up as we scale, especially once we started adding more modules. Some integrations also take more effort than we expected and aren't always as plug-and-play as we'd hope.
The system can get a bit slow as we grow, and some pages with large amounts of data dont display properly on smaller screens, making them harder to use.
column10: Acumatica helps us keep everything in one place instead of juggling multiple systems. It gives us better visibility into our operations and real-time data, which make it easier to make decisions and saves us a lot of time day to day. Easy to Use, Worth It for Visibility and
column28: {"submitted": "2026-04-28T10:25:10.158-05:00", "type": "organic", "region": "North America", "updated": "2026-04-28T10:25:10.634-05:00", "url": "https://www.g2.com/products/acumatica/reviews/12703534", "title": "Time Savings", "primary": "Americas", "switched_from": {"products": [{"id": "148997", "name": "Intuit QuickBooks", "slug": "intuit-quickbooks"}], "reason": "We switched mainly because Quickbooks and our in-house system couldnt keep up as we grew, and the data was too fragmented across platforms.\r\nCompared to Quickbooks, Acumatica gives us much better scalability and deeper operational visibility beyond just accounting. Overall, Acumatica feels more integrated and structured, making it easier to manage finance and operations in one place instead of relying on multiple disconnected tools."}}

column7: I really appreciate Acumatica's automation capabilities and the ability to view data just the way we need to see it. We utilize business events to notify users in real-time or to make real-time changes using import scenarios. The data visualization aspect is also significant for us. After rolling out Manufacturing, we created dashboards that show our line efficiency, allowing us to review our BOMs and evaluate accuracy in labor, which helps us decide on necessary changes.
column8: Right now WMS is below par - we need to implement another software to be able to do license plating, freeze locations, do cycle counting etc.
column10: Acumatica centralizes all our data, automates processes, and allows real-time notifications and updates through business events. We also use dashboards for monitoring line efficiency and adjusting BOMS, enhancing our decision-making. Centralized Efficiency with Automation &

column7: Acumatica offers a wide range of features and capabilities that are very user-friendly. One aspect I particularly appreciate is the flexibility to assign various levels of access to different users within our organization. This has made a significant difference, as it allows our operations team to access the information they need for their projects directly in Acumatica, without having to rely on the accounting team for assistance.
column8: It can be a bit clunky at times, and to get any customization/changes done on the system can be a bit challenging
column10: The approval mapping feature has saved us a significant amount of time and energy, especially since our structure is quite complex with various projects and subaccounts. With Acumatica, AP items are sent directly to the appropriate approving manager, who can easily access all the information needed to review and approve them. Solid Tool with Some Clunky

column7: I like that Acumatica allows us to share the same chart of accounts across all our companies. It's really helpful having all the company data in the same tenant as it makes cross-checking internal invoices easy.
column8: I find the periods/months difficult to read, and the balance sheet and profit & loss don't have a standard template; everything needs to be tailor-made. The initial setup wasn't easy at all, every setting is so specific.
column10: Acumatica consolidates various tasks like budget, invoice approval, and payroll into a single platform, making it our one-stop software. Sharing the same chart of accounts and easy internal invoice cross-checking across all companies has streamlined our processes. Centralizes Finance, Needs

column7: I like the interfaces and the ability to upload entries. I also appreciate the connectivity with Velixo.
column8: It's difficult to use Acumatica to its fullest potential in my organization. The system has cumbersome reporting, so we rely heavily on external financials for reporting. The out-of-the-box reports do not work, and we struggle to address our issues within the system. Existing reports do not run, so we have to use ARM to create our own, and my team is not very savvy beyond Excel. Modules need to be set up individually, which adds too much overhead. Customizations that could improve operations, like dashboards and GIs and AI, require a coding background that my team lacks. It's hard to find a setting toggle or key item, which makes it almost unusable. We spend too much time looking for answers externally. There's minimal support for optimizing the software, and our business branches were set up in a non-GAAP compliant way, which complicates things. The initial setup was poor and we have to deal with these mistakes every day.
column10: Acumatica offers a record-keeping system for AP, AR, and journal entries. Steep Learning Curve, Needs

column7: I appreciate Acumatica's mobile access in a world that increasingly requires EASY access from anywhere. It's a nice change from our legacy system, which had matured to the point where growth was stunted. I also like that Acumatica is a young product with plenty of room to grow.
column8: More reports available out of box at implementation are desperately needed-new customers learning a new system do NOT need to have to learn how to do a GI or master reporting software just to get the data they need. The report designer lacks much to be desired... complete overhaul is in order. Lack of basic out-of-box reports was a big headache and stressor. Knowing I had to reinvent the wheel, to get something I knew other customers also needed, was frustrating.
column10: I use Acumatica for easy access from anywhere, which supports our increasingly mobile workforce and offers a refreshing change from our previous system. Mobile Access and Flexibility Shine, Reports Need

column7: I like Acumatica's intuitive UI, which makes the barrier for entry easy. It's set up well for integrations and customizations, allowing it to feed into and be driven by existing systems. I also find the cost to be very competitive, making the larger more Enterprise ERP options less enticing.
column8: The CPQ functionality is limited. Although there are external systems that can be bolted on to handle this, it would be better if this was native functionality in the tool. There are two major things the current system doesn't handle well. The first is multi-level BOMs. Being able to create BOMs with configurations and stack them on top of each other to create a larger product, while maintaining the ability to configure each subassembly on a sales order or quote is key. The second is the ability to integrate with CAD systems like SolidWorks, where engineers build their models that house the rules for how to update the BOMs as configurations change. Also, much of the necessary data isn't available at initial setup, so we have to standardize and collect it, which makes the process long.
column10: I use Acumatica to have a single source of truth for data, allowing for timely and accurate business decisions. Intuitive ERP with Seamless Integration, Needs

column7: I like that Acumatica is a one-stop-shop for everything from customer management to parts sales, labor tracking, and invoicing. We can create proposals using D-tools, which sync to Acumatica project quotes, then convert to a project where we use service orders and sales orders to track parts and labor. Our projects also have billing tasks built in and a dashboard that keeps track of it all.
column8: I believe the areas where Acumatica could be improved are the taxes and change order/change request processes. The change orders don't track estimated taxes, and in the revenue budget, items can either include tax or be non-taxable entirely, which complicates commission calculations. Also, I found the Microsoft Outlook and Teams plug-ins with Acumatica very difficult to execute thoroughly and successfully. I have not been able to get Outlook or Teams plug-ins to work at all. Additionally, the initial setup of Acumatica was very difficult.
column10: I use Acumatica to track installation labor and recognize revenue over time, manage sales and commissions, and understand opportunities lost. Its integration with d-tools helps create proposals, syncs with project quotes, and facilitates service orders, sales orders, and billing tasks. Comprehensive Solution with

column7: I use Acumatica for all operations like manufacturing, inventory management, financial processing, CRM, and WMS. I love how Acumatica can be personalized to fit the industry I work in and even down to my specific role within the company. The increased visibility of data and the ability to highlight KPIs for all team members are great. It has also streamlined and automated many processes. As someone who oversees data and analytics, having access to real-time data to show different departments how their operations are running and to get ahead of issues is huge.
column8: The Shopify connector I always have issues with. It is not very flexible and often causes extra work for different team members to monitor failed sync records. I think there needs to be more allowance to edit the work flow of each entity. Specifically on the items, in my business we take pre orders for items before we even know if we can secure the seed (due to crop failure). The item status is very strict and does not work the same for the Shopify display and the Acumatica display.
column10: I oversee operations like manufacturing and inventory with Acumatica, which increases data visibility, highlights KPIs, and automates processes for the team. Highly Customizable, Improves

column7: I like that we can use handheld devices in the warehouse with Acumatica. It lets us take and upload photos of the packing slips and material directly to the transactions in real time. This saves me from having to scan, rename, and file each packing slip at the end of the day.
column8: I feel like there is still so much for me to learn with Acumatica. Our biggest hardship in the warehouse is the time it takes to process materials (transactions). It takes several minutes to get items received and onto the production floor. I'm sure this can become more efficient when I learn more on automation. The implementation took a long time, about 1.5 years, and wasn't easy, as it took me away from many of my daily responsibilities as a Warehouse Manager. I think we still needed more time prior to implementation.
column10: Acumatica enhances inventory visibility and uncovers issues with staff behavior. It allows us to upload packing slips in real-time, eliminating manual scanning and filing. However, it can be time-consuming to process materials, but I'm hopeful that learning more about automation will help. Revolutionized Our Warehouse with

column7: I appreciate Acumatica for its simplicity and intuitive design, which makes it easy for basic users to navigate and complete their tasks, speeding up processes and finding efficiencies. I find the flexible UI, dedicated workspaces, and robust user roles particularly useful, allowing me to control user access and declutter workspaces, which helps users focus on their tasks and facilitates cross-functional collaboration across departments like sales and production. I also value how Acumatica has almost eliminated our dependency on Excel, which was a big issue with our previous software. Plus, the initial setup of Acumatica was easy, with the main challenge being cleaning our data beforehand.
column8: The WMS is very bare bones. It has some limitations like the fact you can't lookup an item's alternate locations during the middle of an order pick.
column10: Acumatica provides a better holistic view of our company, simplifies processes, streamlines tasks, and reduces Excel dependence. Its intuitive interface allows easy navigation, and its flexible UI supports cross-functional collaboration, enhancing efficiency. Intuitive ERP with Robust

column7: I like Acumatica's capability for financial controls, project management, time entry, financial reporting, service management, and manufacturing, especially in the construction and services industry. Its scalability and flexibility to adapt as we grow are key benefits for us. I also really appreciate the ability to change major workflows, approvals, and reports with low-code/no-code. Acumatica's generic inquiry tool is amazing because it lets me build almost every report needed for our business, whether it's financial reporting, operational reporting, or tracking records through workflows.
column8: The field services module is probably the weakest aspect that could use more work. There are improvements on the roadmap that definitely solve the issues that we are experiencing. The calendar board for dispatching, for example, has a lot of limitations on scheduling intuitively and lacks the ability to show it as a calendar for field technicians on their mobile phone. It does not sync with other apps like Microsoft Outlook.
column10: I use Acumatica for financial controls and project management. It solves scalability and flexibility issues, adapting to our growth. I appreciate its low-code/no-code capabilities for changing workflows, approvals, and reports, and the generic inquiry tool for comprehensive reporting. Scalable Solution with Customizable

column7: I find Acumatica's reporting feature easy to use, allowing me to create unique reports that are tailored to our needs rather than relying on pre-made reports. I like that Acumatica is continuously growing and advancing, with new advancements every year that keep it ahead of older ERP solutions. The new UI features are particularly valuable to me as they let me design the format of the screens to create a more user-friendly environment, minimizing unnecessary clicks for my end users.
column8: One of my end users' biggest complaints is seeing inventory allocation inside the sales order line screen. They currently have to click into the line details screen to see this information. This is a long process if you have a lot of items on the order. It was a lot of work to convert the data from the old system to Acumatica. Also early on in the implementation process, we had to make decisions on things we did not fully understand, and we just didn't know enough about the full capability of Acumatica to make some of these decisions.
column10: Acumatica makes it easy to find and create customized reports and inquiries unique to us, improving user experience. It's continuously advancing, outpacing older ERP solutions. New UI features let me design user-friendly screens, reducing extra clicks and making navigation intuitive. Powerful Advancements, Minimal

column7:
I find Acumatica to be exceptionally intuitive. When I joined a new company with minimal training, I was able to teach myself how to navigate and use the system with ease. Unlike many other programs that come with frustrating quirks and steep learning curves, Acumatica runs smoothly and efficiently, offering a user-friendly interface that makes adoption straightforward.
The platform is robust and feature-rich. Even after four years of daily use, I’m still discovering new modules and capabilities. In my 20-year accounting career, Acumatica stands out as one of the best—if not the best—software solutions I’ve worked with.
column8: The only module I’ve found to be somewhat less intuitive is the Inventory module. While I don’t use it daily in my role, I’ve noticed it can be a bit tricky to navigate at times. That said, it still performs reliably and offers solid functionality. I’m confident that with a bit more hands-on experience, I’ll be able to master it fully.
column10: Acumatica addresses a wide range of business challenges, but one standout feature is its ability to manage multiple companies and their respective branches with seamless integration. Whether you need these entities to operate in sync or remain entirely independent, Acumatica handles both scenarios effortlessly Four Years In—and
column28: {"submitted": "2025-08-07T15:41:10.134-05:00", "type": "vendor_self", "region": "North America", "updated": "2026-01-27T13:10:50.175-06:00", "url": "https://www.g2.com/products/acumatica/reviews/11508650", "title": "Still Impressed", "primary": "Americas", "switched_from": {"products": [{"id": "390", "name": "Sage Intacct", "slug": "sage-intacct"}], "reason": "Needed a system with more capabilities for a growing business."}}

column7: I appreciate that Acumatica is a big improvement over our legacy system, allowing us to scale our processes and increase the capacity and bandwidth of my team. I find it helpful that it enables us to grow and scale our processes without needing to increase headcount as our business expands. The mass actions through General Inquiries (GIs), screens, and import scenarios have been a huge value add for my team. They allow us to bulk process orders and manage allocations without having to click into individual sales orders.
column8: Occasionally loading speeds on General Inquiries (GIs) and dashboards have slowed down processes for us. Also, there was a steep learning curve because it functions so differently, so it was difficult to learn initially.
column10: Acumatica helps us grow and scale our processes, reducing the need for more staff. Mass actions through GIs, screens, and import scenarios allow bulk order processing, managing allocations efficiently without individual order access. A Steep Learning Curve

column7: I really like the access to data Acumatica provides, especially through the Generic Inquiries (GIs), which allows me to have better access without relying on another group. The potential for more automation is exciting as well; we're slowly working on creating this. The ability to build what I need and value, beyond standard reports like P/L and Balance Sheets, is a big plus. I also want to learn to use Business Events for automation to alert my team on certain processes.
column8: The GIs took time to learn. It would be great if there was a wizard for new users to build even a simple one. Also, the initial setup was moderate. We unfortunately had a firm deadline and we were not able to fully set up Acumatica on Day 1 and are still trying to catch up.
column10: Acumatica gives me better access to data compared to our old ERP, allowing me to create custom GIs without relying on others. I also see potential for more automation, specifically using Business Events to alert my team on certain processes. Valuable Data Access, Needs

column7: I really appreciate Acumatica's flexibility in supporting all types of my business operations. It handles everything from DCAA compliance for government contracting to visibility and reporting for performance testing services. Plus, it manages accounting for our real estate business and supports foreign currency transactions, allowing me to consolidate and translate to USD. The system's ease of use for my employees is also a big plus, and having one system of truth for all my entities helps maintain consistency across all companies. The setup was straightforward, and we went live in just four months.
column8: I find the financial reporting to be less user-friendly, that's why we use Velixo. It would be better if it was easier to build reports.
column10: I use Acumatica as a single source of truth across all entities, ensuring consistency. It supports different business needs, like DCAA Compliance, visibility for testing services, real estate accounting, and handling foreign currency entities. Flexible ERP with Robust

column7: I love how easy the sales orders are implemented and how I can search for any specific word that is in an order detail. What really stands out to me is how intuitive the sales order process is from start to finish. Creating and implementing an order doesn’t feel like a chore; it’s straightforward, clean, and doesn’t require jumping through unnecessary hoops. That alone saves time and reduces errors, especially when things are moving fast. The search functionality is a game-changer. Being able to search for any specific word or detail inside an order means I can instantly find what I’m looking for without digging through multiple screens or relying on memory. Whether it’s a customer note, a product reference, or a custom detail, it’s all immediately accessible. Net result: less time hunting, more time executing, and better visibility across the board. That efficiency is what makes Acumatica genuinely valuable in day-to-day operations.
column8: Some workflows feel more complex than they need to be, especially for new users, and there's a bit of a learning curve. Certain screens aren’t always as intuitive as the sales order side of the system. Reporting and data extraction can also be a challenge, as getting data into a clean, usable format sometimes takes more steps than expected. Performance can feel inconsistent, particularly when running searches or reports with a lot of data.
column10: Acumatica helps locate unknown problems and targets marketing to the right clientele. The intuitive sales order process saves time and reduces errors. The search feature allows quick access to specific order details, improving efficiency and visibility. Intuitive Sales Order Process, but Room for

column7: I use the Acumatica CRM and all of the accounting features related to project accounting, revenue recognition, month-end job costing, and timesheets. I like the stability of the program and, in particular, the flexibility to customize it. I think Velixo is genius and a perfect pairing to get the data out of Acumatica in a form everyone in our company can use without having to change the way they work.
column8: We struggle with revenue projections because we can't group projects together as a program, or create subtasks. We can't use the inventory IDs efficiently for our business. Because we are locked into using the inventory IDs for projecting revenue, we can't get the granularity we need. If we create too many tasks, our guys in the field and in engineering spend too much time on their time cards. The initial setup was awful. Our first partner was not helpful. We are with Crestwood now, but we still struggle.
column10: I use Acumatica CRM and accounting features, eliminating the need for spreadsheets for our financial data. Flexible, Stable, Yet Setup

column7: I use Acumatica for finance matters. One thing I really like about it is that it makes our vendor bill processing more automated, which means less paper. It's also easy to use, which is important for me. What I enjoy the most, though, is the simplicity of running inquiries and reports with Acumatica.
column8: I'm having trouble with multi-branch tax reporting. Our taxes seem to carry balances into the next period, and it's unclear why they're not clearing. This causes our tax reports not to match our trial balances and account details. Also, the initial setup wasn't easy, probably because of a lack of testing on our end.
column10: I use Acumatica for finance matters, reducing paper usage by automating vendor bill processing. It's easy to use, and I enjoy the simplicity of running inquiries and reports. However, it struggles with multi-branch tax reporting, as our taxes don't clear correctly and reports don't match balances. Effective ERP with Room for

column7: I like how everything is in one place with Acumatica, from accounting to sales and inventory. It's great that when other teams use the system, it automatically takes care of some of my work. I appreciate the automations and simplicity of the system.
column8: I'm still new to Acumatica and some of the programming feels complicated to set up myself. I don't have a coding background and sometimes it feels like it requires that level of skills, like setting up GIs and business events. I wish business events and GIs were more user friendly to set up. I could automate a lot more of my work if I knew how to set these up simply. Also, I wish there had been an official training or some sort of resource I could utilize when first getting used to the system.

column7:
I have used many small accounting and larger ERP systems. None work like Acumatica. When I said this system was written to run in the cloud - I really mean it. You can tell the company worked hard with industry experts to plan, design, and then program a software solution. This is not legacy software attempting to run in the cloud.

You can import and export from any system screen. Most common user screens have the ability with one click like sales order but for power users you can easily hit any screen/table you want. 

I required very fancy customer discounts. This was the only system that could handle it.

I have over 100k SKUs and 50k bins. The system didn't complain. No user screens started to choke either.
column8:
I originally purchased a perpetual license many years ago. Unfortunately, those are no longer offered. My annual maintenance price has not changed each year which was a pleasant surprise. This year I was forced to upgrade to the latest version or I would get penalized 25% each month. It was in the middle of our busy season and nobody wanted to go through a major upgrade that could cause issues. I can understand support not wanting to help if we are on an old version but to force us to upgrade or fine us harshly is just down right abuse from a vendor. If that practice doesn't stop then I can see people leaving.

Don't try hosting this software on a virtual server. It will not perform well. Make sure you get a dedicated server. Also don't use the MySQL database. The only one that works is the MSSQL.

column7:
Built from the ground up for cloud. Powerful but easy to use with a fairly simple interface and clean design. Because it's built for the cloud the features are extensible through their entire market place of partners that add amazing functionality. It's amazingly fast too. You would think that it's a native application the way it generates reports and completes robust tasks. 

The feature set is constantly growing because of the yearly release cycle so new features are always on the horizon.
column8: I do wish things like creating reports, big imports and creating widgets and dashboards were more user accessible and that we didn't need to rely on our implementation partner so much. While I like the fact that new features are always being added the 2 releases per year always makes you feel like you're behind or missing out on the cutting edge.
column10:
User accessibility. We have a remote work force. No longer have to work with a VPN because the it's based in the cloud. 

Slightly longer implementation than I would have liked. Though switching ERPs is a pretty significant project and this timeline is really based on your partner.

column7: It isn't easy to pinpoint in a review the high-level areas of Acumatica that are most favorable. Everything is modular, so you can use what you need. Everything is customizable to fit your needs as well. If something you need isn't there and can't be customized, there's a marketplace to find a certified partner to integrate a solution. Already have a solution? You can integrate those with an API connection. There's no downside.
column8: There isn't much to dislike about Acumatica. It gets the job done. In doing so, it's not the most flashy ERP. Their focus is to get the data you need. And as such, they don't spend a lot of time making it look pretty. If you want pretty and don't care about functionality then you're probably not reading this review.
column10:
Before utilizing Acumatica, we were using QuickBooks to take care of our finances. This is great for small businesses. But we were growing so quickly that we needed a solution that would grow and scale with us. We are also an omni-channel company when it comes to our revenue streams. Managing this without an ERP solution is nearly impossible. Acumatica helps streamline this for us and gives us the visibility we needed. 

We're also utilizing Sales Force and our own VMS system. Acumatica allows us to connect to everything and conduct business from a single platform. We're able to handle our finances, manage workflows, sales orders, track customers, manufacturing and much much more. 

Having everything in a single system reduces time spent finding information and increases productivity. Everything You

column8:
Two main issues: 
1) We use the percent of complete method for project revenue recognition. Acumatica does not recognize revenue that is attributed to labor hours (professional services) in real time. Using a schedule to do so at an elected frequency is not ideal as financials are therefore not always up to date. Furthermore, Acumatica is not bound by the dollar limits of the actual project and will over-recognize revenue if actual costs exceed budgeted amounts, requiring manual intervention via journal entries to claw-back the excess revenue. This leads to overstated financials unless and until you review each open project to confirm excess revenue has not been recognized. 
2)Ease with customizing reports and general inquiries is sorely needed. This especially comes into play if the fields you wish to include are not part of the standard report. You cannot filter on a field if it is not an option to begin with. Havin the ability to easily add fields that already exist in Acumatica to canned reports or GI's would greatly improve data analysis.

One minor issue only because it is needed once annually: 1099 reporting. Would be ideal to be able to print and file 1099s directly from Acumatica as well as the 1096. Electronic filing of your 1096 is now required for filers with more than 10 1099's. We currently have to use an outside software program to do this.

column7: We have been using this product since 2014 (version 4.2). The changes each year continue to impress. column10: We had a lot of inventory discrepencies in the past. Acumatica is helping us validate the raw materials when it comes in, use it, follow the movement of locations, & helps us know what we are really using. It helps us better cost our products.

column7:
We tried to roll out another ERP prior to Acumatica, and it failed miserably. In our search for something to replace it, we were looking for something that was user-friendly, cost-effective for our small team, and would expand our capabilities to allow us to grow. We have found it to be just that and are relatively happy with our decision to go with this platform. It's forced us to really think through workflows and streamline our processes as a team, which is helpful! 

We also find it helpful to have visibility into what our entire team is doing. We can see the whole process, from Purchase Orders, to Sales Orders, payments, etc., and have a better idea of item/order statuses without having to shout down the hall to ask.

Get yourself a good VAR because it makes a huge difference! You will rely on them for so much. We were paired with one that works well for our team.
column8:
Acumatica has a lot of incredibly cool features, but some of them require add-ons or customizations. Our general theme in searching for an ERP platform was "If Quickbooks can do it, so should you." For example, the Document Recognition software is really neat, but it's an added monthly charge. That's something that the basic Quickbooks package offers for free. We started our Acumatica journey with a new (to us) payment processor, but had many issues with their integration. We ended up switching to Acumatica Payments, which actually saves us a good chunk of money in CC fees, but in doing so we also lost some functionality and it forced us to upgrade earlier than we had planned to do so. 

I imagine that integration issues are common across the board for ERPs, but we really have been pretty pleased with Acumatica.

column7: I'm into my 11th month using Acumatica and I can honestly say it's more expansive than any other platform I've seen before. I see great potential but...
column8: Acumatica is built with very large, multinational clients in mind. Picture Toyota for example. The kind of large corporation that has an entire building full of IT professionals who will design and customize every user facing screen the average employee might see. This is NOT a turnkey platform. It's the engine hidden underneath whatever glossy exterior you can imagine. The problem is, right out of the box, you don't even have that engine yet. You'll get the parts for that engine. And there's not "some" assembly required. It's ALL assembly is required! The nuts and bolts you need for that assembly are like a giant basket of scrabble tiles each containing a word that only software engineers can understand. You quickly find out your only option is to pay enormous sums of money just to make the engine run. Congrats, you now have the equivalent of a moped and you're trying to drive on the highway. So you pony up and ask them to assemble a car. It arrives but wait.. no wheels? I'm sorry, wheels are extra. I know this is a silly analogy, but it's pretty close. I'm an accountant with 25 years of experience. In all my travels, I've never come across software that's both so vast yet can't do the most basic things when you first install it. Acumatica kinda knew what a balance sheet was but I literally had to map out every single pixel before I could print out anything that resembles one!
column10: Acumatica can do almost anything you want it to, but everything you ask of it will have to be programmed, step by step. Kinda like teaching it how to walk again every time you want a new pair of shoes! Acumatica:

column7: Josh Fischer. I don't need to be sold to, I need technical information to make informed decisions. Josh has been incredibly specific and knowledgeable, as well as organic and friendly. Personally I'm from outside the C# ecosystem, so it's taken an extra bit of effort to get more involved and see past my own inability to extend Acumatica for our specific business processes, but I'm coming around.
column8:
"Open standards" or "standard agnostic" choices. In an effort to remain "database agnostic" Acumatica has default support for the worst database management platform on the planet (MSSQL et al. specifically)

Similarly, by building on the .NET framework you're locked to windows environments. While the general consensus might be critical about introducing the JavaScript ecosystem to the platform, it will open up the ability of customer companies to expand their in house teams (js is objectively easier to hire for)

That being said, what the hell is Aurelia.js? Use the de facto standards, react or angular. Aurelia's banner literally says "Join thousands creating next-generation apps"

There are millions of people using react and angular, and it has global support. Whoever decided on Aurelia probably also thought deno.js was the next JavaScript framework (hint, it wasn't, typescript was the winner)
column10: Dashboards and reporting are ok, but there isn't a particular reason we picked Acumatica except for the low barrier of cost entry.

column7: Acumatica impresses with its user-friendly design and modular structure, facilitating ease of use and adaptability. The implementation process has been seamless, and the platform's customer support is commendable, providing timely assistance. The platform's consistent performance aligns well with regular organizational management, and its extensive features are customizable to meet specific needs. The ease of integration enhances overall efficiency.
column8: While the platform's functionality is robust, improvements in the user interface could enhance overall user-friendliness. Though the implementation process has been smooth, continuous refinements could further enhance the initial setup experience. Customer support has been positive overall, and no significant issues have been encountered in terms of frequency of use. The abundance of features is appreciated, but managing and customizing them can be overwhelming, suggesting a need for simplified feature management. The ease of integration has been satisfactory; however, exploring ways to streamline integration processes further could be beneficial.
column10: In the realm of financial management, Acumatica takes the lead in streamlining our processes. It addresses the complexity of tracking and managing funds disbursed to buying agents, enhancing accuracy in recording transactions and providing transparency in fund disbursement. As a cocoa export company, Acumatica efficiently manages the value of goods supplied, offering a comprehensive overview of supply chain transactions and contributing to accurate financial reporting. column7:
Acumatica provides instant access to the software and a large library of videos and guides to help get started without spending a dime. Download the latest version for a free 2-user access and have it running in minutes for evaluation using the "Sales Demo" data that can be applied at installation. Acumatica corporate and customer peer support is always within reach. With Visual Studio, complex customization can be added easily, and many customer enhancements don't even require visual studio.

The development community continues to grow. When I have issues, I can post on Stackoverflow to get the support of my peers as well as answering questions others ask to improve my own skillset.
column8: I use FIFO costing, and FIFO works a little "too" First In-First Out. When I have a Sales Order with an attached Purchase Order, the costing does not necessarily flow from the PO to the SO inherently. With configuration and clever use of locations, there is work around, but the nature of a SO-PO link should force the costing to flow as "Actual Specific" even when the item is defined as FIFO.
column9: Our legacy system has worked well for a long time, and modern enhancements layered on top have helped with the longevity of the software. However, the aging architecture and the base platform have not kept pace with modern technology. Acumatica allows us to build our proprietary add-ons atop a solid platform with modern technology and tools. Many custom enhancements for us on our legacy system are made into
column10:
Please don't put it off! Download the free software, install it right away, and explore the features. See for yourself what it can do for you. It's free. There are free training videos for technically savvy individuals. You don't have to take my word for it. You need a little bit of time and the willingness to explore for yourself.

Since implementing Acumatica, we have developed in-house customizations to fill in our very specialized business processes ranging from simple 5-minute enhancements without coding to complex solutions with sophisticated event handling. The modern toolset enables streamlining business workflow through automation and validation at entry.

column7: It is a sensational cloud ERP solution; what I like most is its high level of customization and configuration; we have been able to adapt it to the specific needs of our organization, and at the same time, it has also been appropriately integrated with other business and productivity tools to ensure greater efficiency. We can access it from anywhere as long as we have a connection, and its advanced security measures ensure that business data is kept safe. Acumatica has helped us streamline processes and automate tasks while providing real-time transparency of all business operations; it is an unmatched ERP solution.
column8: It is widely customizable, but the process is somewhat complex; it has taken us a significant amount of time to adapt it to the organization's needs, and the costs associated with licensing, customization and maintenance can be very substantial, which ends up being somewhat limiting for small and emerging companies.
column10: It is an indispensable ERP solution in our daily operations; operating in the cloud gives us multiple benefits, such as accessibility from anywhere as long as you have an internet connection, scalability and automatic updates. Its user interface is intuitive and easy to use; it integrates with various business and productivity tools, automates processes and workflows, is widely customizable and has numerous features that help us maintain business management. An unmatched cloud

column8:
1) Forecasting Revenue - Acumatica should calculate the forecasting automatically. When entering the percentage, it should calculate the dollars and vice versa.
2) Billing Retention - Acumatica should create one AIA document for retention instead of a separate entry for each prior billing that has to be released. On large projects, it's easy to lose track of which line items need to be released. Acumatica does not provide an AIA Retention Billing - that's messed up.
3) AIA form - The AIA document is not formatted correctly, so we never use it. Instead, we use an excel form outside of Acumatica.
4) When drilling down into the costs within a task or committed cost, you can't see the vendor name of what was purchased, just a PO or subcontract number. No one wants to open each line item and drill down further to get all the vendor names. We should be able to pick the task code, drill down and get a list, including the vendor/supplier name, dollar value, PO/subcontract #, etc. Right now, we only see the description, dates, Qty/$$, APBL# - can't even open the APBL # listed.
column10: I have not heard of any Acumatica solutions to correcting the revenue forecasting, the AIA formatting, creating one AIA document for retention or better drill down features. Not impressed at with Acumatica -

column7: I'm an Acumataholic! I like that Acumatica allows non-technical users to change screens easily. We can select the fields we want on the screen, sort & re-arrange fields on the screens, in look-up windows and change our navigation menus. I like adding filters to grids and preview lists, even in the "look-up" screens. I like the search capabilities and the friendly community! I like being able to create database snapshots (backups) without needing SQL Server. I like the way different documents and screens "Cross-populate" each other: the ability to see the Bills on a Check that paid them, see the Check on the Bill that was paid, see a collection of change orders right on the related project, see vendors with prices on inventory items I've bought from the vendors.
column8: After attaching a file, I often want to delete it, and I'd like to have the 'trash can' right THERE on the Files list instead of opening up the separate File Maintenance tab and then closing it again. In the case of an error message, I would like the messages on Tools-Trace to be easier to understand.
column10: Today in the 2022 R2 Launch, Acumatica provided the ability to track installation units on projects. For example: In addition to the total # of Bricks and the $$ price of the bricks on the project budget, we will be able to see # bricks installed by each employee each day on the timecards. One of our customers has requested this.

column7: The most helpful part of Acumatica is the ability to get at the data. This comes into play for daily processing between staff where one person can enter information and attach backup and another can get the details on the related project without needing to ask for it. Being able to drill down on the information from screens you are familiar with also gives independence to staff without their need to learn how someone else does their job. The ability to structure reports and extract data through tools like generic inquiries also allows one to get the information out in the format you need for outside purposes like uploading data to a bank for positive payor payments or provide details for the annual audit.

column7:
Acumatica has all the business administration features that major ERP systems have but, the features in Acumatica have much more depth, flexibility and extensibility. The large depth of the features covers all common and complex business scenarios in Finance (GL, Cash Management,, AP, AR), Sales (Quotes, Sales orders, shipments, returns, ..), Purchasing (Product Requisitions, Purchase Orders, Product receipts, Billing, Payment processing), Inventory (items, assemblies, kits, warehouses, lots, bins, serial #, ..), Project Management, Fixed Asset Management, HR (payroll, employee Management), Tax Management. The flexibility is enabled by the configuration capability in Acumatica. The end user can easily set the parameters for eg. inventory replenishment, item costing, GL account preference for stock, non stock and service items, etc. This is done without any programming. The extensibility enables the end user to add fields to a screen or form or report without any programming. The end user can embed Sharepoint sites, any webpages, and any self created wiki pages. 
In addition to this, the Web REST Services API and SDK of the Acumatica ERP platform enables an Independent Software Vendor to add industry specific applications such as Discrete Manufacturing Management, Retail Management Systems, Warehouse Management Systems, Parcel carrier systems, Tax Management systems, Property management systems, Construction Management systems, Field service Management systems, etc. All the above industry specific applications are already today integrated with Acumatica ERP, and running at many businesses with great success.
The pricing is not per user but, per suite and module, and per computer resource (cores). If I add some sales people, I don't have to pay extra.
The support of Acumatica is phenomenal. Their online training and guides are really thorough, accurate and complete. Yes you will have to study hard but, you will get to master an ERP that is so powerful that you will be able to do everything you want. 
This is a true cloud ERP that can run in your private cloud, in a public cloud or on your servers. The software performance can easily handle many thousands of transactions a day. 

Benefits

The benefits are as follows:
. I can now really master the cash flow situation of my company, and if there are problems, I can spot them ahead and identify them at the level needed. I avoid the typical cash shortage crises when payroll is due. 
. I avoid overstocking and understocking items
. I can now easily do a cost break-even analysis so that when I need to decide to hire more people, I can find out from my cost break-even analysis how much more sales I need to generate to cover the cost of added personnel.
, The customer and vendor portals enable my company to interact with my customers and vendors on a daily basis. This avoids so much miscommunication, misunderstanding and dragging support issues. This empowers and engages my customers and vendors. Embedding Sharepoint sites helps us a lot.
column8: There is a lot to learn but, no pain no gain. After reaching the 80% pass level in the online course, you bet that you know ERP, accounting, CRM, purchasing, sales management, inventory management, HR, etc.

column8:
The order entry, shipping, and customer portal are all quite cumbersome. Their "device hub" software for printing does not work on Macs so it is an annoying process to print packing slips and shipping labels. 

I wish all of the order entry fields were visible at once instead of having to go through different tabs.

Some of the international shipping carrier integrations generate forms that could cause customs problems, we have brought this up to Acumatica and they don't seem to understand or care that they are incorrect. 

The auto packaging function only takes total volume into account, (not length, width, and height) which is not useful unless you are shipping water or something that will conform to the box size...

The customer portal is very rudimentary, basically just a catalog. You can't see a photo of the product until you actually click on it, which can be confusing if you have multiple products with similar SKUs like we do. Many of our customers have complained that it is difficult to use.

column9:
We did not have any problems to solve in regards to the why we switched software, it was a benefits issue from the get go. 

The version of Acumatica that we purchased is a cloud based system which allows all of my people to be able to work from home or while away on business (yes we could do it before but we had to log into the office server, this is a lot easier). 

The additional benefits we have gained are numerous; Speed in processing our outgoing shipment was vital and with a click of the mouse we can have a batch of 20 or 30 or 100 ordered processed in the time it took us to do 2 with our old system. The search functions are great and not hard to understand and use. Having integrated email has saved us tons of time and allows us all to be able to see what has been sent to a customer when dealing with quotes or question.

column7: <v ->My name is Raul.</v> I'm actually a data science at, I'm currently a data science engineer. And I would say, I would rate the product around eight. And my company is in the industry of oil and gas, but we mostly deal with software products. So right now, our company is actually using it for time and expense management. So we use it for daily timing the log. So your day-to-day work. And also for any expenses that you already have, any out expenses or something that's not, say any unusual expenditure that you did. So you may want to just come up and just upload it there. So just to get the investments. So I understand the product is doing quite well in terms of capturing the whole receipts. And say, so let's say you are uploading an expense, so you can just go and upload any number of receipts. And you can also view them at, out on the go. So that was a good feature that I want because I can just see everything at the same screen. And I don't have to just go to another tab and see, so that was good. And apart from that, it makes it easy for us to manage the time and log the expenses because we have an harmonic view of the whole week. And normally, everyone just go at the end of the day and try to just log that. So it is pretty good, the whole experience I would say. So I would say Acumatica doesn't have any preferences sort of things. So let's say I'm using to view something daily. So for me, it's like I'm using the same code to log my expenses and say log my daily activities, but Acumatica is not intelligent enough to recognize this. It just help me just get them on the go. So I have to every time go and select that particular project code for which I want to log my hours. So that was one thing. And then another, I suppose it ask you to also log. So it is built in a way that it recognizes end of the month. So every time, it's kind of a little bit frustrating that you have to go and click on end of the month for a Sunday that is not even working. That you have to go and mark that yeah, you work and you have zero expenses. And then one more thing, if you don't have anything, so let's say you don't have any expenses, you just go and enter zero. But in the description field, it's kind of mandatory. I think it can just recognize it as optional or maybe give it a warning that you have not given any description. So sometimes I have to go and enter. And I know what they are. So that is kind of a little bit I would say they can improve on. So I've also used other tools for time and tracking. So I would say Acumatica is still better than the ones that I've already used in the past. But the recommendation I would give to anyone is just to look at the business case that they are trying to solve and how quickly. So if it is just a time tracking and daily expenses, it is quite a good tool. I've also used tools like Conquer. So there they have a more visual, say kind of aspect to it. So you can also try to get a trial and just try to compare it with a different person.

column7: Acumatica is highly flexible and easy to customize which helps quickly figure out what works and what doesn't. It's convenient to use on the go through its mobile application which makes it perfect for remote working. Powerful reporting is easy to manage with a simple data integration and the outputs can be conveniently exported in a tabular format for further analysis. The UI is streamlined and sleek, making it easy to navigate. The product documentation is informative and the platform boasts a powerful API that can be easily integrated with other applications in your organization's ecosystem.
column8: Keep in mind that customizing and extending the platform requires technical resources with programming experience. I wish there was a low-code option for customizations.
column10: The core benefit of Acumatica is consolidating business processes such as customer management, order management, customer support, financial reporting, etc. into a single platform in order to reduce licensing costs and operational spend on training employees to master multiple platforms. In addition, your overall business becomes a lot more efficient when data and processes are in a single location.

column7: Acumatica is great for keeping track of our financials. The amount of costing methods is excellent, and the reports that our team can create are helpful. Since our team uses this for construction, project setup is reasonably easy for us. The customization of dashboards and GI tables gives us the ability to tailor the application to our team's needs. The operations team is able to navigate the desktop and mobile app with only a few hiccups. When the mobile app is working correctly, the ability to enter expenses on the go has been great. With the different approval settings available, getting expenses in the system is fast. This has helped our accounting team greatly, as they have been able to close out the monthly books quicker than they ever have before.
column8: The construction management side of the software is not perfect. There are a lot of areas that still need improvement. The field service tool is one of those areas. The field report section is not all there yet. The ease of entering information is not great. In a field service tool, team members need to be able to quickly enter information and reference their logs. Coming from Procore, this service is severely lacking. The mobile app can be clunky at times. We have had many issues with team members being unable to access certain areas of the application, as well as having issues with getting into the app.
column10: We have solved the problem of our ops team not having direct access to financials. Acumatica allows our team members to be involved in the financial aspects of a project. This is helping with accountability, as well as delivering projects at a better margin. Great

column7: Ecommerce is such an important part of our business and Acumatica is in front of the train when it comes the momentum towards ecommerce away from traditional sales methods. We made the switch to Acumatica to streamline order information from Amazon and Shopify and while also making our distribution, wholesale, and manufacturing orders much easier to manage. The minute adjustments that you can make to EVERYTHING is so helpful, but it's a double-edged sword. It's overwhelming to work through all of the user roles to get them to the right level of functionality/productivity while also blocking sensitive information from certain users. Pretty much any field/task can be blocked, but it's unclear until testing how the disabling of a field will muck things up down the line. You really have to test out all of the bugs!
column8: We are on the smaller end of Acumatica customers being a manufacturer/ecommerce business of 20 people, so this was a very expensive/scary move for us and we lacked some of the technical know-how to move in the right direction during the initial stages of development. We had create and re-create tables and work flows multiple times because we didn't get the hand-holding we were expecting during the design process. If you get a development consultant that takes the time to understand your products during the early stages, then this should not be as much of a problem for you. My advice is that you should have somebody on staff that really understands relational database structures before launching on your Acumatica journey as this 60% of the work. When all is said and done, Acumatica is a very powerful tool that is worth the investment for even a small business.

column7: Completely cloud based ERP solution is a big improvement over our prior in-house server solution. System is very easy to use, which makes training a breeze.
column8: Increasingly, our wholesale customers are asking for a slick, Amazon-like user experience where they can go online to place their own orders, track their shipments and see their order history online. The current CRM solution doesn't meet this need - its too cumbersome and not slick enough. We would like to see Acumatica address this -- either natively within the Acumatica software itself, or through a connector to some other cost-effective and easy to manage and launch B2B/online solution.
column9: We've been able to eliminate the need for some of the 3rd party connections/solutions that we needed with our previous ERP (Sage); the built in Acumatica solutions work. This has simplified our in-house IT needs.
column10: Give Acumatica a hard look. It is a good and reasonable solution for small and mid-size companies.

column7: Acumatica's ERP is built on top of an awesome framework that allows you to customize just about everything that you could possibly want. Most solutions always require some sort of tweaking and customization to support business processes. In quite a number of cases with other ERPs this tends to create odd ball workarounds that either half work or are confusing. Acumatica ERP was built from the ground up to be customized and engineered to flexible at every level.
column8: The one dislike that I have is being addressed on the product roadmap, which is migration over to .Net Core which will open up a whole host of options for development, testing, and production hosting. Now that the .Net platform is growing up and evolving into the .Net 5 world of cross platform support, it's great to see the Acumatica taking notice and staying true to the future proof mantra.
column9: We use it touch just about every touch point in our business. If you need to change something that is key to your business, Acumatica makes it possible. We work better, deal with changes faster, and manage things easier due to the flexibility that Acumatica provides. Super
column10: Picking an ERP is a BIG deal and your decision is going to be with you for a while. Experience has taught me no two people or two companies fit into the same mold when it comes to their data/ERP needs. Besides looking at a feature list and checking off things one by one, consider what features are not there. Ask yourself what if your process changes, workflows that may need to be changed, or what would happen if all of a sudden I need to customize something. Make sure you pick a platform that can change and grow with you. Acumatica knocks it out of the park.

column7: We are just starting to implement this ERP System. I am very impressed by the capabilities which match all of our needs. I think the easy search abilities and training is astounding. I am impressed by the Lot/Serial Tracking. Overall this system is extremely agile, you can change things fairly fast, and it can adapt quickly to robust changes within our business.
column8: As a buyer, I wish the purchasing methods were more robust. I am nervous about inventory optimization with the limited purchasing methods. Additionally the importing scenarios is a bit challenging and can be tedious.
column9: We want one robust system which stores all of our orders, inventory, distribution, and accounting. The main purpose is to align our finance and inventory systems. Additionally to create and manage more master data item, better reporting, and creating standard dashboards. Despite the purchasing not being as robust, it will still create a lot of efficiency for our organization.
column10: If you are looking for a robust system and is constantly innovating, on the cloud, and is fairly easy to understand.

column8: Hmmm... a much harder question. Sometimes my logic and the system logic don't mesh. The only example I can think of is that it took me a few transactions to realize that the Purchase Receipt was not the genesis of the GL transaction. But it just shows how strong the back-end of the system is. The Inventory Receipt transaction is automatically created from the Purchase Receipt (depending on item setup). The Inventory Receipt creates the GL effect. (Logical once you think about it.)
column9: Again, as an implementer, the problems solved are varied. One of the solved problems most often mentioned is the increased productivity by using one integrated system vs. many systems. Easiest ERP to
column10: Know your business processes before you begin implementing. Have a dedicated implementation team of at least two people. Make sure the executives of your company are in sync with the implementation and will provide quick decisions. Allow time for project planning and communicate the changes with the entire company.

column8: What I dislike is the fact that error messages within Acumatica are not always easy to understand. I have found that many do not tell you exactly what is wrong and how to fix it. For instance, when doing imports, sometimes you get an error message with the details of what from the import template is wrong but sometimes it will just say that there is an error. When you have many columns on our template, this could take more time than necessary to figure out what's wrong.

column10: I recommend Acumatica to any organization that wants to manage its finances effectively and safely, is a software designed especially for business world is a program that has an intuitive platform that facilitates the work of your entire company, powerful to generate reports, manage inventory and track expenses that arise in each project, would not recommend Acuamatica for distribution and manufacturing companies because it depends on third party add-ons to implement ERP and finally has an unusual price structure that forces us to investigate much is their different plans

column7: I find that the overall interface is easy to work with. I like that I can search for GL accounts by name, and I like the detail of the construction edition features. Sub jobs, sub accounts, cost codes, and nonstock items are helpful features to track in this edition. I also do love the various reports I can run. I absolutely love the copy and template features. These are helpful when you have the same bill each month or want to keep the same format for future invoices. It is also helpful for when you have to reverse a mistake; rather than reentering all of the details, you can simply copy the previous document, paste, and fix the mistake. So much easier than a lot of other customized ERP systems.
column8: I have found that not all reports are customizable. I wish I could customize aging reports to be customer or customer-range specific and invoices. The transaction detail report is not customizable in the ways I need, but I wish it was. I also find that the user roles are very convoluted. I still haven't figured out how to give someone access to add new account in the chart of accounts. I also wish I could amend 1099 fields on a previously closed invoice, as sometimes I don't have the 1099 information at the time of receiving a bill.
column9: I love that I can export any page to excel, which helps me sort out anything I need to report or sort out that which is too specific for a general report. Going from our old accounting team's Sage 300 software, Acumatica makes so much information easier to find and amend for whatever I am being asked to report. Tracking project costs, comparing them to the budget, and the drilling down features are all very helpful and have made my project tracking easier.
column10: My organization made the mistake of pushing me to implement without training, which I wouldn't recommend. Do the training because although it is easy to use once you get the hang of it, it has so many features that it requires a deeper understanding of what needs to be set up for your specific books from the beginning. Save yourself the headache, and do the training. It will be worth it.

column7: After reviewing multiple ERP options, we chose Acumatica for the relative ease of implementation that allowed us to walk before we run. Another advantage we saw was the ability to expand our use of the system as we grow as a company. We found Acumatica ERP system to be very user friendly with an excellent Help system. We were able to learn how to do a lot of task on our own by using Help files.
column8: We found Report Designer to be the least user friendly function compared to all others. We were able to do what we needed but it was not as intuitive as with most other tasks.
column10: Prior to using Acumatica, all our entities were using different accounting systems and consolidating them required emailing Excel reports, adding them up manually and took a long time. By implementing Acumatica for all our subsidiaries, we shortened our month-end close from 2 weeks to 5 days and avoid errors.

column8: The reporting tools leave a lot to be desired. The Generic Inquiry and Report Writer are a bit rudimentary. I'd like to see more cohesion between them, like an easy export from Generic Inquiries to the report writer. Another issue is exporting images to excel. It'd be great to run catalogs out to excel, manipulate the data, then use that same catalog as an import back into the system.
column9: Time-Phased Inventory management has become a lot easier. I've pulled hours of time off of very skilled people by seeing my inventory in time-phased sets. I can project demand and supply with relative ease. Once I export that data out to excel, I can then make projections on our inventory purchasing requirements. This has been one of the greatest benefits to our company. Super
column10: Hit the AUG forums if you get stuck, it's free and Tim Rodman is super helpful and polite. No question is too small there. Of course, if you need help with your C# coding, visit StackOverflow and use the Acumatica tag on your posts. Lots of free development help there for customizing your platform.

column7: Acumatica keeps a solid focus on growth and integration. The platform has excellent capabilities when it comes to using your data how you want to use it, whether that is brining in external data our driving external actions based on data in Acumatica.

column10: Be open to adjusting your workflow to fit well within Acumatica. These don’t have to be large changes, but force fitting old methodologies can hamper your success on the platform.

column7: The framework. There is a great depth of configurability in the system with GIs, Reporting, Dashboards and more. The real star in my book though is the customization platform. Being able to utilize Acumatica's developer tools and caching framework to get the system to meet your unique needs as a company, and then having the GIs and Dashboards treat your information as a 1st class citizen is where Acumatica shines.
column8:
I find it difficult to submit issues to Acumatica. I don't want to involve my VAR every time, and I am not always looking for a patch, but for the issue to get addressed in an upcoming release. The feedback site is decent, but sometimes there are very specific scenarios where the software does not perform as expected. This isn't necessarily an issue with only Acumatica, all ERP systems have this same flaw in my book..

The upgrade process can be a bit of a bear due to how rabidly the Acumatica system is being developed. There is so much to keep track of between releases, it can become overwhelming.
column9: Acumatica is acting as our company ERP and Accounting system. Having the system available via browser is a great benefit for remote users. Acumatica is the best ERP

column7: The reporting capabilities of Acumatica are immensely helpful. The dashboards, generic inquiries equip us to make better and faster management decisions. It also gives significant flexibility as to the visibility permissions of team members. Setting up the system was very difficult and complicated, but if done well, is well worth the time and effort.
column8:
1. Setting up the system is difficult and complicated. There has to be higher levels of competency for administrators and the project team; and new people would find it difficult to learn the internal structure and set-up making the company dependent on the original administrators. 

2. Local support is not yet strong enough, and there is not enough knowledge online to easily google solutions to problems. I feel that strong channel partner support is very critical and something that Acumatica needs to strengthen to grow the install base in the Philippines.
column9:
1. There is more information flowing through the company, allowing decentralization while at the same time empowering people to make better and more well-informed decisions.

2. Saves time and effort - automation allows us to save on time and effort in gathering information and coordinating, when people have information at their fingertips.
column10: Acumatica is a very powerful tool when set up well. I recommend having a good, highly-organized and intelligent core team who is familiar with the company's business and are forward thinkers. Make sure you get a solid channel partner as local support, because you will surely need it. Maximize the use and make the system work for your company.

column7: Industry specific for construction with familiar terms & concepts. Solid interfacing options overall using web hooks and the screen api. Screens are consistent and overall easy to use. column8: Smaller ecosystem to find help if implementation team is not providing clear answers compared to larger ERP/Accounting systems. The provided REST API (oData), while promising, needs more maturity and wrappers to be truly effective and enterprise quality. Would like to see a better experience around general inquiries overall. I have not yet seen any solid reporting solutions or data modeling/warehouse out of the box and would like to see more growth there.

column7: The greatest strength of Acumatica is its technical flexibility and its platform-centric design. The customization tools have allowed us to modify the system to suit our business without extensive cost and the data tools (generic inquiries in particular) are very powerful. We have also generally found Acumatica intuitive, easy-to-use and easy to teach. We have also been happy with the continued development of the platform in the 2+ years we've used it.
column8: There are some more advanced pieces of functionality in our area of business, such as replenishment forecasting, that are not particularly strong, although we have general found ways to meet these needs nonetheless.
column9: We use it for all of our sales, purchasing, order-taking and financial operations (with a standalone WMS for fulfilling orders and receiving stock). column7: I like that the platform is online and estensible. Acumatica is continuing to evolve and grow on a regular basis, almost at a clip that is difficult to keep up with from a business perspective.
column8: Acumatica is a monolithic application, something that I would love to see deconstructed and rebuilt around a microservices architecture. The physical server requirements for the system fluctuate throughout the day and because of that we are required to purchase hardware to support our toughest loads. Ideally the system would scale up or down depending on demand. I would also love to see less scheduled actions and more event-driven actions. The idea that I have to schedule how often email is sent from the system, and can only schedule it to occur as quickly as every minute, is not consistent, in my opinion, with what Acumatica represents technologically speaking.
column10: We are replacing our custom-built ERP system and separate accounting system (MS GP) with Acumatica. Combining the two systems will be a significant leap forward for the organization.

column7: Coming from Sage Software, it looks like Acumatica will allow us to grow to scale and provides more in depth analytics of our sales and operations. It's not as simple to use, but I see this software helping us create operating efficiency and management of our warehouse. I also can't express how useful it is that this software is built on a browser platform. This allows me to work efficiently from anywhere whereas I would always be stuck in my office.
column8: It seems like there are multiple steps for simple processes such as sales orders, purchase orders, etc. Coming from Sage, everything was a simple 1-2 step process. Acumatica seems to be a 3-5 steps for all of these similar tasks. Also, if you go into a print screen and forget to save your work, you'll lose everything by clicking on back. The action tab needs to be available in the print screen to prevent data loss. The sales order process is also quite cumbersome. The training is a key aspect in learning how to use Acumatica. We are in the training process right now and will hopefully go live in two weeks.
column9: We are working to create operating efficiency, sales analytics to help with purchasing, and warehouse management, along with integrating a CRM and customer portal. One of our biggest issues in the business is with warehouse mispicks. This upsets customers and Acumatica will help us integrate into lot tracking and also factoring in warehouse management at some point.
column10: Know what your getting into and understand that you'll have to clean up your whole database before going in. It's not as simple as importing your data if you don't have your data properly formatted and organized. Acumatica can be a great tool for growing your business, but you'll have to go through the growing pains in setting up all your data properly for import and the training process to ensure all users have proper knowledge in how to use the software. The hardest thing for long time employees to accept was going through new daily processes.
