column7: The Financial Reporting capabilities and flexibility is excellent. The cloud-based (offsite) data storage is a space saver for us as we scan all payables documents to the cloud, so no document retention requirements. The remote server aspects save us much time, effort, and expense with server management, patches, updates, and licensing costs. We run multiple companies on the same platform, which extends our investment.
column8: Some features are just far too complicated or overkill for smallish business. Due to the nature of our business, we use only a subset of the many, many features. Payroll has become so intertwined with HR functions, that we must offload that to payroll-specific providers. It is a fairly expensive product, given our limited needs, but exit costs are pretty high.
column9:

column7: As a user of both Acumatica and also Quickbooks, I can see just how important it is to have a proper ERP that fits your company. Our company found that we had outgrown Quickbooks, and wanted a program that went the extra mile to organize some of the more perplexing areas in our business. Better decisions lead to better performance in every company, and we have found that Acumatica is the perfect tool to guide us to better decision making,
column8: Acumaitca can be monotonous at times, especially when it comes to correcting minor mistakes that are made. When a document is released in Acumatica, there is no room for error, and no chance to make tiny corrections. You are punished by having to reverse out the incorrect document, and then add the same document you worked on back in with the proper corrections. The ability to copy documents helps, but it can be time consuming if a lot of mistakes are made.
column9: Acumatica has helped us solve many business problems. We needed a record keeping program that logged time for our developers in order to take advantage of a research & development tax credit that is very beneficial to companies in our field. Now that we have Acumatica, that information is easily available. and we can store any supporting documentation conveniently within the system. This alone saves us thousands of dollars each year.
column10: Highly recommend Acumatica, but make sure to work with those necessary to properly implement the system into your company, You want to make sure it is done correctly.

column7:
1 - Unlimited user licensing - One of the key decision factors was that I didn't have to pay per license. All of the other competitors I looked at, you pay per user, and for us, it got to where we weren't going to be able to afford it. 
2 - Customer / Vendor portals - Then customer portals and vendor portals were big for me. Other competitors, for if you wanted customer or vendor portals, you bought another user license for that. Not for each customer or vendor, but it was another user license that you bought.
3 - Responsive design - I like the fact with the responsive design of Acumatica, I am not dependent to access my business software from a PC. No matter what device I am on (iPad, Android, iPhone, or any other device I have full access to my software resources. The fact that data being entered in to Acumatica at the office and the sales team in the field have instant access to the data helps us stay on top of business and proactively provide great customer service.
4 - Help within Acumatica - This was one of the deciding factors to go with Acumatica. Within the software, wherever you are you can click on the help and it brings up a help page explaining the current page you're on and what each form means and that's helped me out tremendously.
5 - Uniform design throughout the software - The software GUI is broken into Modules - Company, Finance, Distribution, Configuration, System, etc. Within each module the layout of where to enter data is same through out each module. This shortens the learning curve during employee training. 
6 - Search capabilities - Any where in the program you can enter what your looking for in the search bar and the results are displayed like a Google search. 
7 - Outlook plug-in - Within Outlook, when you receive an email from a customer, you can click on the Acumatica plugin (within Outlook) and it will query Acumatica and let you know if the contact is in Acumatica or not. If not, it is easy as clicking the add button to enter that contract in Acumatica. This is all done within Outlook. No cutting and pasting from Outlook to your CRM software.
